meaning: vi.争论,争吵,口角vt.辩驳,放牧n.口角,吵嘴
examples: Don't wrangle with others over trifles.
The two of them began to wrangle the moment they got talking.
Diplomats wrangle over targets for 2020 and 2050.
But anything other than a straight in-or-out question may result in a damaging wrangle.
But asking what price the nuclear electricity will be is proving a much tougher wrangle.
